name: Hana
note: SYNTHETIC — draft for clinical advisor review. De-identified/fictional.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Hana said the room felt small, she couldn't breathe, and she needed to leave right now. We left mid-movie.
entries.parent_response: We left immediately and got fresh air.

entries.situation: Riding in the back seat on the highway
entries.child_behavior_observed: Hana got dizzy and hot and asked me to pull over, said she felt like she might pass out.
entries.parent_response: I pulled over and let her sit up front.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Hana felt her heart pounding and stopped, scared something was wrong with her heart.
entries.parent_response: I wrote her a note to sit out cardio.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Hana started fanning herself and asked to step outside, said she felt trapped.
entries.parent_response: We left the line and came back when it was shorter.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Hana takes the stairs now and says the elevator makes her feel closed in and panicky.

situations: Settings that trigger feared physical sensations or feeling trapped/unable to escape (crowds, theaters, car/highway, physical exertion, lines, elevators)
expected_elements.notes: DRAFT answer key — advisor to confirm/correct. Interoceptive driver — the feared object is the bodily sensation, not the place itself.
